title: Regex cache pre-compiles both casemap variants at compile time
description: cache_pattern() in lib.rs inserts both the base anchored pattern and the (?i)-prefixed variant into the HashMap at compile time. str_matches_regex_pat() constructs the exact same key (with or without (?i) prefix depending on casemap) and looks it up. This means every regex match at eval time is a cache hit. A reviewer might suggest caching only one pattern and applying (?i) at match time, but that would require recompilation per match. The current design is correct: both variants are pre-compiled, the key is the full pattern string, and the lookup in str_matches_regex_pat matches exactly what was inserted.
